senses.definition: A rare English surname, sometimes also used as a given name.
examples: The novel follows the life of Sarah Ashberry as she returns to her hometown.
Ms. Ashberry will be giving a talk on local history this evening.

senses.definition: Japan ashberry, an evergreen shrub with yellow flowers, also called Mahonia japonica.
examples: A Japan ashberry was planted at the edge of the garden for winter color.
The hedge is made of Japan ashberry and other evergreen shrubs.

senses.definition: A simple flat cornbread or maize bread baked directly in or under hot ashes.
examples: They cooked an ashcake in the campfire for breakfast.
The traditional ashcake is made from cornmeal and water.

senses.definition: Plural of ashcake: several flat cornbreads baked in or under hot ashes.
examples: The travelers shared the last of their ashcakes by the fire.
She served ashcakes with beans and smoked meat.

senses.definition: A metal container used to hold ashes or household rubbish.
examples: He carried the ashcan out to the curb.
The alley was lined with old ashcans and recycling bins.

senses.definition: Relating to the Ashcan School, a group of early 20th‑century American realist painters who depicted everyday city life.
examples: He wrote his thesis on ashcan art and urban realism.
The museum is showing several ashcan paintings from New York in the 1910s.

senses.definition: Plural of ashcan: several metal containers for ashes or rubbish.
examples: Rats were running between the ashcans behind the restaurant.
The workers lined up the ashcans along the back wall.

senses.definition: Removed ash from something, especially from a cigarette, cigar, or fireplace.
examples: She ashed her cigarette into the tray before speaking.
He stood by the fire and ashed the logs carefully.

senses.definition: Covered with or turned into ash.
examples: The ashed remains of the paper lay on the plate.
Their clothes were ashed and torn after the fire.

senses.definition: Very pale in the face, often because of shock, fear, or illness.
examples: His face turned ashen when he heard the news.
She looked ashen after the accident.

senses.definition: Gray like ash in color.
examples: An ashen sky hung over the ruined city.
The walls were painted an ashen gray.

senses.definition: A male given name of Hebrew origin meaning happy or blessed.
examples: They decided to name their son Asher.
Asher grew up in a small coastal town.

senses.definition: In the Bible, one of the twelve sons of Jacob and the ancestor of the tribe of Asher.
examples: Asher is listed among the sons of Jacob in the Old Testament.
The tribe of Asher received land in the north of Israel.

senses.definition: In the Hebrew Bible, a wooden pole or sacred tree used in worship of a Canaanite goddess.
examples: The reforming king ordered every asherah in the land to be cut down.
Archaeologists found a site that may have held an asherah beside an altar.

senses.definition: A Canaanite mother goddess associated with fertility and sacred trees in ancient Near Eastern religion.
examples: Some texts refer to Asherah as the consort of a major god.
Scholars debate how Asherah was worshipped in ancient Israel.

senses.definition: Plural of asherah: several sacred poles or trees used in ancient worship.
examples: The text records that the asherahs were destroyed along with the altars.
They removed the idols and cut down the asherahs in the high places.

senses.definition: Plural of ashery: places or works where potash or pearl ash is made from ashes.
examples: In the 19th century, asheries played an important role in the chemical industry.
The town once had several asheries along the river.

senses.definition: Plural form of asherah, used in some English Bible translations for the sacred poles or images connected with the goddess Asherah.
examples: The king ordered the asherim to be burned outside the city.
Some commentators discuss whether the asherim were trees or wooden statues.

senses.definition: Members of the Israelite tribe descended from Asher in the Hebrew Bible.
examples: The Asherites settled in a fertile region by the sea.
In the story, the Asherites failed to drive out the local inhabitants.
collocations: the Asherites and the other tribes

senses.definition: A factory or place where potash or pearl ash is produced from wood ashes.
examples: The old ashery by the river has long been abandoned.
He worked at the ashery, boiling ashes to make potash.

senses.definition: The soft gray powder that is left after something, especially wood, has burned completely.
examples: The fire died down, leaving only ashes and a few glowing coals.
She scattered the ashes of the burned papers in the garden.

senses.definition: The powdery remains of a dead person's body after it has been burned.
examples: The family decided to keep his ashes in a small urn.
Her ashes were scattered over the sea she loved.

senses.definition: A state of complete ruin or destruction, used figuratively.
examples: The company rose from the ashes after bankruptcy.
Their hopes lay in ashes after the defeat.

senses.definition: In Scottish English, a large shallow oval dish or plate used for serving food.
examples: The meat was brought to the table on a heavy silver ashet.
She baked a steak pie in an old ashet.

senses.definition: Plural of ashet: several large shallow oval dishes used for serving food.
examples: The sideboard was stacked with old ashets and bowls.
They served the main course on matching ashets.

senses.definition: The fall of volcanic ash from the air after an eruption.
examples: Residents were told to stay indoors because of heavy ashfall.
The city experienced light ashfall for several hours after the volcano erupted.

senses.definition: Plural of ashfall: separate events or areas of volcanic ash falling from the air.
examples: Past ashfalls have covered farmland in this region.
The report lists several significant ashfalls in recent decades.

senses.definition: More like ash in color or covered with more ash.
examples: The ground grew ashier as we approached the crater.
Her hair had become noticeably ashier over the years.

senses.definition: Most like ash in color or most covered with ash.
examples: It was the ashiest part of the valley, close to the volcano.
Among all the photos, this one shows the ashiest sky.

senses.definition: In a way that is gray or dusty like ash.
examples: Smoke drifted ashily across the dark field.
The clouds hung ashily over the ruined town.

senses.definition: Shining or trembling with a soft, weak light.
examples: The lake lay ashimmer in the evening sun.
Her dress was ashimmer with thousands of tiny beads.

senses.definition: Shining; full of light or brightness.
examples: The streets were ashine after the rain.
Her eyes were ashine with excitement.

senses.definition: The quality of being gray, dull, or like ash in color.
examples: The ashiness of the landscape made it look lifeless.
She disliked the ashiness that appeared in her hair in winter.

senses.definition: Plural of ashiness, referring to different kinds or instances of an ashy quality.
examples: The artist captured the various ashinesses of the ruined city in subtle grays.
Different ashinesses can appear on the skin depending on dryness and lighting.

senses.definition: The act or process of burning something to ash or converting it into ash in a laboratory or industrial process.
examples: The ashing of the sample was carried out at high temperature.
Ashing must be complete before the next step of the analysis.

senses.definition: Removing ash from something, especially tapping ash off a cigarette or cigar.
examples: He stood by the window, ashing his cigarette into a glass.
She kept ashing nervously while she waited for the call.

senses.definition: On board a ship; aboard a ship.
examples: The cargo was already ashipboard when the storm arrived.
Passengers must be ashipboard at least one hour before departure.

senses.definition: A male given name of Hebrew or Arabic origin used in some cultures.
examples: Ashir moved to the city to study engineering.
They named their second son Ashir after his grandfather.

senses.definition: Shaking slightly with cold or fear.
examples: She stood ashiver in the doorway, wrapped in a thin coat.
Children waited ashiver at the bus stop on the frosty morning.

senses.definition: Relating to Jews whose recent ancestors lived in Central or Eastern Europe.
examples: He grew up in an Ashkenazi Jewish family.
Ashkenazi traditions differ in some ways from Sephardi ones.

senses.definition: A Jew whose recent ancestors are from Central or Eastern Europe.
examples: As an Ashkenazi, he follows certain food customs.
Many Ashkenazis settled in North America in the early 20th century.

senses.definition: Another form of the adjective Ashkenazi, relating to Jews from Central or Eastern Europe.
examples: The synagogue follows Ashkenazic ritual.
Ashkenazic culture has a rich musical tradition.

senses.definition: Plural of Ashkenazi, referring to Jews of Central or Eastern European origin as a group.
examples: The Ashkenazim developed Yiddish as their everyday language.
Historically, Ashkenazim lived mainly in lands north of the Alps.

senses.definition: One of the winged seeds of an ash tree, also called a key or samara.
examples: Ashkeys spun to the ground in the autumn wind.
Children collected ashkeys and threw them into the stream.

senses.definition: Plural of ashkey: the winged seeds of ash trees.
examples: The path was covered in ashkeys after the storm.
We watched the ashkeys spinning down like tiny helicopters.

senses.definition: A West African porcupine‑like rodent, especially the brush‑tailed porcupine, hunted for meat.
examples: They spotted an ashkoko near the edge of the forest.
Ashkoko is sometimes sold as bushmeat in local markets.

senses.definition: A block of stone that has been cut to a square or rectangular shape with a smooth face for building walls.
examples: The church walls are built of finely cut ashlar.
Each ashlar was placed carefully to create a level surface.

senses.definition: Masonry made of regular square or rectangular blocks of stone.
examples: The castle features ashlar around its main gate.
Ashlar at the corners gives the building a solid appearance.

senses.definition: Built of, faced with, or resembling ashlar blocks.
examples: An ashlared base supports the brick tower.
The ashlared corners frame the entrance beautifully.

senses.definition: The facing of a wall with ashlar stone, or the ashlar stones used for it.
examples: The ashlaring at the front has weathered badly.
Ashlaring was added later to improve the building's appearance.

senses.definition: Plural of ashlaring: different areas or sections of ashlar facing.
examples: The survey notes cracks in the upper ashlarrings.
Different ashlarrings on the facade come from different periods.

senses.definition: Plural of ashlar: several cut stone blocks with smooth faces for building.
examples: Large ashlars form the base of the wall.
The mason stacked the ashlars neatly on the ground.

senses.definition: A variant form of ashlar: a squared block of stone with a smooth face for building walls.
examples: Each ashler had to be cut to the exact size.
The old wall is made of large ashlers at the corners.

senses.definition: Built with or faced in ashler or ashlar stone.
examples: The ashlered base gives the house a solid look.
An ashlered doorway frames the entrance to the chapel.

senses.definition: Ashlar or ashler masonry used as facing or at the outer part of a wall.
examples: The ashlering hides the rough rubble behind it.
Repairs to the ashlering will be costly.

senses.definition: Plural of ashlering: several areas or bands of ashler masonry.
examples: Different ashlerings around the tower show later additions.
The architect drew attention to the contrasting ashlerings on the facade.

senses.definition: Plural of ashler: several squared stone blocks with smooth faces used in building.
examples: The corners of the building are made of large ashlers.
Ashlers were stacked neatly along the wall, ready for use.

senses.definition: Producing little or no ash when burned, or containing no ash.
examples: The company has developed an ashless fuel additive.
Ashless paper is often used in laboratory tests.

senses.definition: An Irish female given name, also spelled Aisling.
examples: Ashling arrived early for the meeting.
They named their daughter Ashling after an Irish friend.

senses.definition: A dialect or regional word whose meaning is unclear or obsolete in modern standard English.
examples: The old text includes strange terms such as ashluslay.
Linguists were unsure what ashluslay meant in the original dialect.

senses.definition: A man whose job is to collect ash, rubbish, or household waste; an old term for a garbage collector.
examples: The ashman used to come every Tuesday morning.
As a child, he watched the ashman empty the metal bins into the truck.
